page: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the c3-32 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory performance is robust, showing strong (top 25%) results in large-block read and write bandwidth tests, as well as in the PassMark memory score. Decompression tasks via bzip3 and text processing also register in the top 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high-speed NVMe storage offer a solid resource density for compute-heavy tasks without the overhead of hyperthreading. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a lower memory-to-CPU ratio, which may limit memory-intensive operations. The 2 Gbps network bandwidth is a baseline limitation to consider for highly distributed systems.

This instance is suited for compute-bound workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and strong single-threaded performance. Appropriate applications include compilation tasks, text processing, physics simulations, lightweight LLM inference, and web serving workloads that do not require massive memory footprints.
description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.3 GHz with one thread per core, it is paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. The instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth in large-block operations, though its multi-core benchmarks and 2 Gbps network bandwidth align with average tiers. With a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, it offers a balanced, non-hyperthreaded hardware profile for compute-intensive tasks. This configuration is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, physics simulations, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized KVM server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A dedicated-core compute instance featuring high-speed NVMe storage and strong single-threaded performance for processing-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores without hyperthreading
32.0 GB system memory and 400 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Strong memory bandwidth for large-block read and write operations
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and physics simulations
